text:
Market today: Mixed macro but chipmakers are holding up. The big story is Mag 7 losing $2.3T on AI spending jitters - that's your universe. The save is that investors are still backing chipmakers specifically, which is why your semi names aren't cratering. Oil noise and Iran/Hormuz headlines are background static for you.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one screaming for attention - it's a 3x leveraged semi ETF that decays daily, and sitting $36 above your $200 stop isn't comfortable on margin. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - tiny size now but manage it manually, margin amplifies any gap-down hard. SMH (-0.69%) is minor but worth watching given the AI spending jitter headline.

Watch: Whether SMH can hold above $600 as the AI spending narrative develops. SOXL's $200 stop - if semis get a real flush, that level gets tested fast on margin.

Bottom line: Protect SOXL and SPCX first - everything else is fine, but those two can hurt you disproportionately on margin.
plays: SOXL: Consider tightening your stop from $200 toward $215-220 given -7% loss and 3x leverage decay. A bounce to $250+ is a trim opportunity, not a re-add.
SPCX: Keep position small. No data to trade off - if it breaks $150 stop, exit without debate. New IPOs on margin are traps when they turn.
No new buys today - scanner has CRWD ACCUMULATE 8/12 at entry 742.17-741.42 (stop 740.31) but you already hold it at +3.26%; adding here is premium-chasing. Hold what works.
